description: Normal-road lateral-crossing emergency that mirrors the existing left-side truck/threat cases, but places a pedestrian on the nominal right-side escape corridor. Ego travels straight; a truck/van crosses from the left/front-left on a deterministic collision course set by TTC and lateral speed. The right-side pedestrian offset is swept to make the escape corridor range from blocked to marginally usable.
hypothesis: For left-origin lateral threats, rightward drift should dominate only when TTC is very short and the right pedestrian gap is adequate; when the escape-side pedestrian gap is small, the law should suppress drift-right and prefer hard braking/hold-lane despite the lateral threat.
